purpose: Reconcile Shopify subscription-contract and billing-attempt webhooks into durable MMG entitlements, billing cycles, package windows, Thank-you order links, and Customer Portal state without trusting webhook payloads as the final subscription authority.

verification_contract.hmac_input: exact raw request body before JSON parsing
verification_contract.authoritative_reload: Reload the SubscriptionContract through the Shopify Admin GraphQL API after HMAC verification and before entitlement mutation.

idempotency_contract.payload_collision_rule: The same webhook ID with a different SHA-256 payload hash is rejected.
idempotency_contract.processed_duplicate_rule: Return HTTP 200 without applying the event again.
idempotency_contract.retry_rule: Failed retryable deliveries may reclaim the same webhook record and re-run reconciliation.

reconciliation_contract.stale_event_rule: Ignore an event when the persisted contract revision is newer, or when revision IDs are equal and the persisted Shopify triggered-at timestamp is equal or newer.
reconciliation_contract.customer_identity_rule: A provider contract cannot move to a different Shopify customer ID.
reconciliation_contract.plan_change_rule: A valid canonical variant change updates the entitlement plan for the authoritative period; existing historical cycles retain their original capacity.
reconciliation_contract.cycle_creation_rule: Create at most one cycle for each authoritative current-period start and only when the contract is active and both period boundaries are valid.
reconciliation_contract.first_cycle_rule: The first package of the first entitlement cycle is customer-selected, uses manual recovery fallback, and may open immediately for 48 hours.
reconciliation_contract.future_package_rule: All later packages are scheduled-package-review windows with a 48-hour review period and auto-confirm-current-selection fallback subject to existing server revalidation.
reconciliation_contract.weekly_rule: Create exactly four package windows at offsets 0, 7, 14, and 21; never create a fifth package in a five-week calendar month.
reconciliation_contract.pause_and_terminal_rule: Paused, failed, canceled, or expired contracts cancel future scheduled entitlement cycles without revoking already delivered ownership grants.
reconciliation_contract.thank_you_link_rule: Link a verified pending Thank-you order record to the durable entitlement when shop domain, origin order ID, and plan code match.
reconciliation_contract.transaction_rule: Entitlement, contract snapshot, order link, billing attempt, cycle, windows, and entitlement audit event commit or roll back together.

billing_attempt_contract.success: Persist the successful attempt and allow idempotent current-period cycle creation after authoritative contract reload.
billing_attempt_contract.failure: Persist the failed attempt and billing failure metadata; do not fabricate a new paid entitlement cycle.
billing_attempt_contract.challenged: Persist the challenged state for support and reconciliation visibility without granting new entitlement capacity.
billing_attempt_contract.terminal_precedence: A recorded succeeded state cannot be downgraded by a later duplicate failure or challenge for the same idempotency key.
billing_attempt_contract.customer_file_access_rule: Existing ownership grants remain durable; access to subscription-selected files still requires delivered-window authorization.

privacy_and_security_contract: Never process the JSON body before raw-body HMAC verification.
Never accept the shop domain, contract identity, customer identity, product identity, plan identity, billing period, or entitlement capacity from browser input.
Never persist the Shopify app client secret or Admin API access token.
Never persist the raw webhook body.
Never expose provider contract IDs, billing-attempt IDs, webhook IDs, access tokens, payload hashes, or reconciliation event payloads to the storefront.
Use parameterized SQL and a database transaction for reconciliation mutations.
Return HTTP 200 for processed duplicates and permanently noncanonical events so Shopify does not retry them indefinitely.
Return a non-2xx response only for retryable processing failures.
release_gates: The Shopify app has protected subscription API access.
The deployed app has the read_own_subscription_contracts scope.
The canonical product, three variants, shared selling plan, and runtime GIDs are provisioned and verified.
The app-specific webhook manifest is merged into the deployed shopify.app.toml and released.
The endpoint is routed through the deployed Kairos runtime.
The Shopify app client secret is available only to the server-side HMAC verifier.
A secure per-shop Admin API token provider is connected to the authoritative contract gateway.
Migrations 001 through 005 are applied to production PostgreSQL.
Create, update, success, failure, challenged, duplicate, stale, replay, payload-collision, wrong-shop, wrong-version, invalid-HMAC, and Admin API outage cases pass.
Monthly, Bi-weekly, and Weekly subscriptions create exactly one, two, and four windows with two assets each.
A five-week calendar month never creates a fifth Weekly package.
The Thank-you handoff resolves from activation-pending to the first-title selection state after reconciliation.
The Customer Portal reflects active, paused, failed, canceled, and expired membership states without exposing provider internals.
No live webhook registration or entitlement mutation occurs before explicit production release approval.

subsequent_components: Live Shopify provisioning and end-to-end deployment
